arts.ttl: 'I wanted to renew and play for Wolves'
arts.sum: Having held positive conversations with head coach Vitor Pereira, Mosquera is continuing to provide a positive influence on the group off the pitch, and is now itching to get back playing in front of the supporters of the club he’ll call home until 2030. It’s a really good opportunity, because I need to get back and I have another chance to play here.

That form readied him for Premier League football, and Mosquera started all of the Old Gold’s first five outings of this season before suffering injuries to his MCL and ACL in a challenge at Aston Villa in September. The Colombia international, who first joined the Old Gold back in 2021, is currently recovering from a long-term injury layoff, but will remain at the club until 2030, with his contract including a further 12-month option.
